This advisory has been withdrawn because it is a duplicate of GHSA-q99w-vh6v-q3v7. This link is maintained to preserve external references.

## Original Description
OpenClaw before 2026.5.26 contains an authorization bypass vulnerability where a surviving pairing-scoped device session can re-establish node token authority after revocation. Attackers with a paired device can regain WebSocket node-level access without renewed approval, weakening revocation controls and maintaining unauthorized access longer than intended.", "ghsa_id": "GHSA-wrmq-9fc4-gwwj", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-06-18T13:03:03Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-wrmq-9fc4-gwwj", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-wrmq-9fc4-gwwj"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-06-16T19:17:01Z", "published_at": "2026-06-16T21:31:57Z", "references": ["https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/security/advisories/GHSA-q99w-vh6v-q3v7", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-53843", "https://www.vulncheck.com/advisories/openclaw-node-token-revocation-bypass-via-pairing-scoped-device-session", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-wrmq-9fc4-gwwj"], "repository_advisory_url": null, "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw", "summary": "Duplicate Advisory: Pairing-scoped device session could restore revoked node token authority", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-18T13:03:04Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-wrmq-9fc4-gwwj", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": null, "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "openclaw"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 2026.5.26"}], "withdrawn_at": "2026-06-18T13:03:03Z"}
